message:
chore(beads): close bd-vvmd.4.1, bd-vvmd.4.2, bd-vvmd.4.5 after disk-pressure implementation

Update the beads issue tracker to reflect completed work in the disk-pressure
resilience workstream (bd-vvmd.4).

Why these issues are being closed:
The disk-pressure monitoring, policy integration, and capabilities reporting
work has been implemented and validated. Closing the corresponding tracking
issues keeps the issue state consistent with the codebase.

Issues closed:
- bd-vvmd.4.1 (status: open -> closed): "Decide storage_ballast_helper
  integration mode with ADR" -- completed with ADR-006 documenting the CLI
  adapter integration decision and safety/rollout/observability constraints.
  Close reason: "ADR completed: docs/adr/006-storage-ballast-helper-integration.md"

- bd-vvmd.4.2 (status: open -> closed): "Integrate disk pressure monitor +
  ballast policy engine in daemon" -- completed with the disk_pressure module,
  daemon startup wiring, worker state integration, and scheduler preflight
  gate including unit/integration tests for healthy/warning/critical/telemetry-gap.
  Close reason: "Implemented disk pressure monitor/policy integration in rchd
  with scheduler/status wiring + tests; validated via rch exec check/clippy/tests"

- bd-vvmd.4.5 (status: open -> closed): "Extend worker capabilities/health
  reporting with storage pressure details" -- completed with pressure metadata
  fields in WorkerStatusInfo, WorkerCapabilitiesInfo, status issue generation
  for Critical/TelemetryGap states, and contract serialization tests.
  Close reason: "Extended worker capabilities/status with pressure metadata +
  confidence/age fields; added status contract tests and scheduler consumption
  coverage"

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -410,11 +410,11 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","title":"Add exhaustive unit/integration tests for RepoConvergence internals","description":"Add deep unit and integration coverage for repo convergence internals: adapter response parsing, drift-state transitions, bounded retry behavior, policy allowlist enforcement, and worker-selection handoff semantics. Include deterministic mocks for repo_updater failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover adapter success/failure taxonomy (timeout, malformed JSON, partial result, auth/network failure).
- State-machine tests cover `ready/drifting/converging/failed/stale` transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Integration tests verify `bd-vvmd.3.2` + `bd-vvmd.3.3` behavior under missing/stale repo conditions.
- Tests assert fail-open local fallback semantics when convergence cannot be proven within budget.
- Structured logs for each test include scenario id, expected decision, actual decision, and remediation code.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:32:22.268118209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.692347414Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.533185718Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.742878308Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.958451949Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:31.168562531Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.692259600Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added to close a quality gap: repo_updater integration previously had strong E2E coverage but no explicit deep unit/state-machine test bead. This prevents regressions in convergence logic and adapter parsing.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","title":"Define secure credential flow for repo convergence operations","description":"Define secure credential and identity flow for repo convergence actions on workers (keys/tokens/host validation), including rotation, least-privilege scope, and failure handling. Prevent convergence failures caused by auth drift while keeping security posture strict.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration contract specifies auth mechanism(s), secret source, rotation process, and permission boundaries.
- Worker-side credential validation and host identity checks are explicit and testable.
- Failure taxonomy distinguishes auth/authorization/host-verification errors with remediation guidance.
- Convergence operations fail closed for security violations but preserve RCH fail-open for compilation execution fallback.
- Unit/integration tests cover expired/revoked/invalid credentials and host-mismatch scenarios with structured logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:45.401554181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T00:50:00.750469744Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T00:50:00.750451109Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["policy","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.071872224Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.282336508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: repo convergence failures are often auth/identity problems in practice; adding explicit credential lifecycle design prevents silent drift and flaky remote readiness.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4","title":"Workstream: Disk Pressure Resilience via storage_ballast_helper","description":"Purpose: prevent build failures from full disks by integrating `/data/projects/storage_ballast_helper` policy logic into worker maintenance and scheduling decisions. Add proactive pressure detection, safe reclaim actions aware of active builds, and admission control that avoids selecting workers likely to fail from low space.

## Success Criteria
- Pressure monitor + policy engine classify worker disk risk with stable, low-noise signals.
- Reclaim actions are conservative, auditable, and safe for active builds/high-value caches.
- Scheduler admission control consistently avoids high-risk workers while preserving fail-open behavior.
- Disk-pressure recovery tests demonstrate prevention and recovery across unit/integration/E2E paths.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:00.828438642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.502538791Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["disk-pressure","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","storage","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.853550290Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.305201835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.502437331Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":133,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Safety principle: storage remediation must be pressure-aware and build-aware. Reclaim operations that can invalidate active builds or high-value caches are disallowed unless policy-approved. Selection/admission control are first-class prevention mechanisms.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Storage reliability policy: treat disk pressure as a first-class scheduling signal, not just cleanup after failures. Reclaim order must preserve active builds and high-value caches, and actions must be conservative/auditable. Admission control should steer builds away from risky workers before no-space errors happen. Recovery must never rely on destructive broad deletion, and must preserve fail-open semantics for user workflows.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:06Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.4.1","title":"Decide storage_ballast_helper integration mode with ADR","description":"Evaluate direct crate integration vs CLI adapter for storage_ballast_helper. Document tradeoffs (latency, coupling, observability, upgrade risk), choose approach, and define safety constraints for reclaim operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ADR compares crate-integration and CLI-adapter modes across latency, coupling, safety, and upgrade risk.
- Decision includes explicit failure containment and rollback strategy.
- Chosen mode defines clear interface contract and ownership boundaries.
- ADR documents observability hooks required for disk-pressure decisions.
- Approval criteria include sign-off from reliability and operations perspectives.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.680083453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:24.993947252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adr","design","rch-rel-2026q1","storage_ballast_helper"]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","title":"Integrate disk pressure monitor + ballast policy engine in daemon","description":"Embed pressure telemetry and policy evaluation in rchd to detect low-space conditions before failures. Must emit policy decisions and current pressure class for each worker.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Daemon integrates pressure monitor and ballast policy engine with bounded polling cadence.
- Integration exposes normalized pressure states and confidence levels for downstream scheduler use.
- Failure handling differentiates telemetry unavailability from true pressure risk.
- Policy decisions are logged with measured disk evidence and policy-rule provenance.
- Unit/integration tests cover healthy, warning, critical, and telemetry-gap scenarios.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.930877709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:25.252834646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","monitoring","rch-rel-2026q1","storage_ballast_helper"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.594260372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":150,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Pressure monitor expectations: classify worker storage into policy bands with hysteresis so scheduling decisions are stable. Sampling and decisions must be cheap enough for frequent evaluation. Emit both raw metrics and interpreted policy state so operators can understand why a worker was selected or skipped.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:28Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.4.1","title":"Decide storage_ballast_helper integration mode with ADR","description":"Evaluate direct crate integration vs CLI adapter for storage_ballast_helper. Document tradeoffs (latency, coupling, observability, upgrade risk), choose approach, and define safety constraints for reclaim operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ADR compares crate-integration and CLI-adapter modes across latency, coupling, safety, and upgrade risk.
- Decision includes explicit failure containment and rollback strategy.
- Chosen mode defines clear interface contract and ownership boundaries.
- ADR documents observability hooks required for disk-pressure decisions.
- Approval criteria include sign-off from reliability and operations perspectives.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.680083453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T03:03:46.291914376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T03:03:46.291895661Z","close_reason":"ADR completed: docs/adr/006-storage-ballast-helper-integration.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adr","design","rch-rel-2026q1","storage_ballast_helper"]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","title":"Integrate disk pressure monitor + ballast policy engine in daemon","description":"Embed pressure telemetry and policy evaluation in rchd to detect low-space conditions before failures. Must emit policy decisions and current pressure class for each worker.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Daemon integrates pressure monitor and ballast policy engine with bounded polling cadence.
- Integration exposes normalized pressure states and confidence levels for downstream scheduler use.
- Failure handling differentiates telemetry unavailability from true pressure risk.
- Policy decisions are logged with measured disk evidence and policy-rule provenance.
- Unit/integration tests cover healthy, warning, critical, and telemetry-gap scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.930877709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T03:41:28.775950309Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T03:41:28.775931454Z","close_reason":"Implemented disk pressure monitor/policy integration in rchd with scheduler/status wiring + tests; validated via rch exec check/clippy/tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["daemon","monitoring","rch-rel-2026q1","storage_ballast_helper"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.594260372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":150,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Pressure monitor expectations: classify worker storage into policy bands with hysteresis so scheduling decisions are stable. Sampling and decisions must be cheap enough for frequent evaluation. Emit both raw metrics and interpreted policy state so operators can understand why a worker was selected or skipped.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.3","title":"Implement safe reclaim actions aware of active builds and cache value","description":"Implement prioritized cleanup/reclaim logic that protects active build artifacts and high-value caches while reclaiming sufficient space. Include guardrails to prevent over-deletion and preserve deterministic behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reclaim policy never targets active build workdirs or explicitly protected cache regions.
- Reclaim candidates are ranked by safety/value heuristics with deterministic ordering.
- Execution supports dry-run preview and emits auditable before/after disk-state evidence.
- Remediation ladder respects bounded action budgets and safe abort conditions.
- Tests validate no-destructive behavior under concurrent build and low-space stress conditions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.137737455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T01:11:52.776991003Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","rch-rel-2026q1","safety","storage_ballast_helper"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.797491745Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":219,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Safety hardening landed in cache cleanup path: (1) worker eligibility no longer risks lock-across-await stale reads, (2) reclaim command now guards deletion with base-path confinement and recent-activity skip window to avoid deleting actively-mutating cache trees, reducing likelihood of transient dep-graph missing-file failures under load. Added coverage in cache_cleanup tests including command-marker assertions and idle-threshold behavior; full workspace tests green.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:11:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","title":"Add scheduler admission control based on disk pressure risk","description":"Integrate pressure state into worker selection so risky workers are deprioritized or temporarily excluded. Must support local fallback without blocking user workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scheduler admission gate uses pressure risk signals and reservation estimates before worker selection.
- High-risk workers are rejected or deprioritized with explicit decision reasons.
- Admission logic preserves fail-open local fallback when no safe worker remains.
- Decisioning includes hysteresis to avoid oscillation under near-threshold pressure.
- Integration tests verify stable selection behavior across pressure transitions and concurrency.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.347332313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:25.794365074Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["rch-rel-2026q1","reliability","scheduling","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.000644521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.201981307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.728929334Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","title":"Extend worker capabilities/health reporting with storage pressure details","description":"Publish richer storage metrics and pressure reasons in capability payloads and status endpoints. Ensure signals are stable enough for automated scheduling decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker capability payloads include stable storage-pressure metrics and normalized status categories.
- Reported fields are cardinality-safe and suitable for automation consumers.
- Health reporting includes last-sample age and confidence metadata.
- JSON/schema contract tests prevent accidental field drift.
- E2E tests validate operator visibility and scheduler consumption of reported storage state.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.550718025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:26.051782384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capabilities","health","rch-rel-2026q1","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.403191927Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","title":"Extend worker capabilities/health reporting with storage pressure details","description":"Publish richer storage metrics and pressure reasons in capability payloads and status endpoints. Ensure signals are stable enough for automated scheduling decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker capability payloads include stable storage-pressure metrics and normalized status categories.
- Reported fields are cardinality-safe and suitable for automation consumers.
- Health reporting includes last-sample age and confidence metadata.
- JSON/schema contract tests prevent accidental field drift.
- E2E tests validate operator visibility and scheduler consumption of reported storage state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.550718025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T03:50:18.867262157Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T03:50:18.867242921Z","close_reason":"Extended worker capabilities/status with pressure metadata + confidence/age fields; added status contract tests and scheduler consumption coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capabilities","health","rch-rel-2026q1","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:16.403191927Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","title":"Add unit/integration/E2E tests for disk-full prevention and recovery","description":"Build comprehensive unit/integration/E2E tests for disk-pressure prevention and recovery using shared harness tooling plus targeted unit fixtures. Cover imminent-full, full, post-recovery, and active-build contention states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests validate pressure classification and reclaim policy decisions.
- Integration tests validate scheduler admission behavior under pressure transitions.
- E2E tests validate recovery paths and routing away from risky workers before no-space failures.
- Failure-path logs include pressure class, reclaim decisions, scheduling outcomes, and fallback reasons.
- Tests prove no destructive broad cleanup behavior is used in recovery flows.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:06.763125660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.286366419Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","rch-rel-2026q1","storage","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:19.810888070Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:20.019238678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.286184518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.739381980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","title":"Add predictive disk headroom estimator + reservation model","description":"Implement predictive disk headroom model and per-build space reservation estimates so scheduler avoids workers likely to run out of space mid-build. Use closure/planned artifact signals where available and conservative fallback heuristics otherwise.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Headroom estimator computes minimum required free space with confidence bands.
- Scheduler admission logic uses estimator output to reject/deprioritize risky workers before execution.
- Reservation accounting avoids over-committing shared worker disks under concurrency.
- Detailed logs include estimated requirement, observed free space, confidence, and decision path.
- Unit/integration tests validate estimator behavior across small/large builds and concurrent workloads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:45.665066966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37.310100833Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","rch-rel-2026q1","scheduling","storage","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.521905011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:14.312802517Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":163,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: disk-full incidents are best prevented before scheduling. Predictive headroom estimates reduce mid-build failures and avoid wasted remote cycles.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.5","title":"Workstream: Stuck-Process Detection/Remediation via process_triage","description":"Purpose: address remote worker degradation caused by hung or orphaned compilation processes. Integrate `/data/projects/process_triage` to identify pathological process states, execute bounded remediation policies, and feed health signals back into worker selection so unstable nodes are deprioritized or quarantined.

## Success Criteria
- Detector/remediator pipeline achieves high precision with bounded, auditable actions.
- Periodic and on-demand triage loops are operational and exposed via status surfaces.
- Unresolved process debt influences worker scoring with stable recovery hysteresis.
- Process-triage tests verify safety boundaries, low false-positive behavior, and recovery outcomes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:01.024580281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.366160512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["processes","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","stability","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.054257607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.503601592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.702430032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.366021632Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Safety principle: process remediation must be bounded and auditable. No broad kill patterns; only targeted actions derived from correlated build lifecycle evidence. Worker selection penalties reduce repeat incidents while preserving throughput.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Process triage philosophy: prioritize precision over aggressiveness. Detection must correlate process state with known build lifecycle/ownership to avoid false positives. Remediation ladder is bounded (TERM then KILL where allowed), fully logged, and policy-gated. Remaining process debt feeds worker health penalties so degraded workers are deprioritized until stabilized.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:09Z"}]}
